title: quisqualis littorea
intro: Quisqualis littorea, also known as the Rangoon creeper or Queen creeper, is a flowering vine native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, among others. Herbal preparations of Quisqualis littorea include teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
